proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_078 (a continuous variable with range 30-90 and mean ~65, consistent with patient age in years) is positively associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_057 (ordinal with values 0/1/2, consistent with ECOG performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
feature_007 is constant across all patients and contributes no variance to pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import pearsonr; pearsonr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = 0.856 between feature_078 and pfs_months (p ~ 0). Binned means rise monotonically from 0.01 mo (age 30-36) to 7.82 mo (age 84-90) — a roughly linear +0.13 mo per year of age. Direction is opposite to the typical clinical expectation that older patients fare worse, but the signal is overwhelming and dominant in this dataset.

Mean PFS falls from 4.68 mo at feature_057=0 to 3.49 mo at feature_057=1 to 2.38 mo at feature_057=2 — a stepwise decrement of ~1.15 mo per ECOG level. Pearson r = -0.389 (p ~ 0). Strongly consistent with ECOG performance status.
feature_007 has a single unique value (0) across all 50,000 patients; correlation with pfs_months is undefined and the feature carries no information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_013 (continuous, mean 42.7, max 3622, heavy right-skewed — consistent with serum PSA in a prostate cancer cohort) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_009 (continuous, mean 3.80, range ~2-5.5 — consistent with serum albumin in g/dL) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher feature_006 (continuous, mean 3.84, max 24.6) is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Univariate Pearson r = -0.153 (p ≈ 3e-260). After adjusting for age (feature_078) and ECOG (feature_057), each unit of feature_013 reduces pfs_months by ~0.0027 (t = -116, p ~ 0). Direction confirmed.
Univariate Pearson r = +0.123 (p ≈ 1e-166). In the adjusted OLS each 1-unit increase in feature_009 lengthens pfs_months by ~0.48 mo (t = +115, p ~ 0). Direction confirmed.
Univariate Pearson r = -0.143 (p ≈ 1e-225). In the joint multivariable model the adjusted coefficient is -0.077 mo per unit (t = -148, p ~ 0). Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_051 = 1 (a binary indicator with ~55% prevalence) have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_051 = 0.
feature_051 = 1 is associated with substantially higher feature_013 (PSA-like) values than feature_051 = 0, suggesting feature_051 marks a more advanced or treatment-resistant disease state.
Higher feature_067 (integer values 6-10, consistent with Gleason score) is associated with shorter pfs_months independent of age and ECOG.

analyses.code: ttest_ind by feature_051; sm.OLS adjusted for age + ecog
analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.03 vs 3.51 (feature_051 = 0 vs 1). After adjusting for age and ECOG, feature_051 = 1 reduces pfs_months by 0.541 mo (t = -90, p ~ 0). Direction confirmed.

Mean feature_013 = 11.96 in the feature_051 = 0 group vs 67.83 in the feature_051 = 1 group — a ~6-fold difference. This confirms feature_051 acts as a marker of high-tumor-burden / advanced disease in this cohort.

Adjusted Gleason coefficient is -0.0073 mo per unit (t = -2.4, p = 0.018). The adjusted effect is statistically detectable but clinically negligible (Gleason 6 vs 10 differs by only ~0.03 mo). Within each ECOG stratum, mean PFS varies by <0.08 mo across Gleason 6-10. Direction is correct (higher Gleason worse) but magnitude is far smaller than ECOG, age, or feature_051.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_109 = 1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_109 = 0 patients.
Patients with feature_039 = 1 (binary, ~10% prevalence) have longer pfs_months than feature_039 = 0 patients.

feature_109 and feature_039 act synergistically: patients with BOTH features = 1 have substantially longer pfs_months than patients with either feature alone, beyond an additive expectation. Either feature alone provides little to no benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Crude mean PFS 3.83 (feature_109 = 1) vs 3.73 (feature_109 = 0), Pearson r = +0.0149, p = 8.8e-4. The marginal effect appears positive, but in a model that includes both feature_109 and feature_039 plus their interaction, the main effect of feature_109 alone is +0.003 mo (p = 0.68) — i.e. essentially zero in the absence of feature_039.

Crude mean PFS 3.83 (feature_039 = 1) vs 3.73 (feature_039 = 0), Pearson r = +0.0141, p = 1.6e-3. As with feature_109, the apparent univariate benefit is driven entirely by the subset of patients receiving BOTH features — the main effect of feature_039 alone in the interaction model is -0.002 mo (p = 0.73).

analyses.code: OLS with feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_109*feature_039 interaction; cell-mean check
analyses.result_summary: Cell means of pfs_months (n in cell): neither = 3.74 (40,435); only f109 = 3.67 (4,467); only f39 = 3.67 (4,569); both = 5.22 (529). The OLS interaction coefficient feature_109 × feature_039 = +1.438 mo (t = +66.6, p ~ 0) while both main effects are ~0. The combination delivers ~1.5 mo of PFS that neither feature alone provides — a striking synergy. Direction confirmed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 (categorical with values white/black/hispanic/asian/other, consistent with self-reported race/ethnicity) is associated with pfs_months: at least one race category differs from the others.
feature_045 (categorical with values medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured, consistent with insurance type) is associated with pfs_months: at least one insurance category differs from the others.

analyses.code: f_oneway across feature_018; OLS with race dummies adjusted for age+ECOG+feature_051
analyses.result_summary: Crude one-way ANOVA of pfs_months by race: F = 0.20, p = 0.94. Group means cluster tightly (asian 3.80, black 3.74, hispanic 3.74, other 3.75, white 3.74). After adjusting for age, ECOG, and feature_051, every race coefficient remains non-significant (|t| ≤ 0.9, all p ≥ 0.38). No detectable association between race and PFS in this cohort. Hypothesis NOT supported.

analyses.code: f_oneway across feature_045; OLS with insurance dummies adjusted
analyses.result_summary: Group means: medicaid 3.74, medicare 3.75, private 3.74, uninsured 3.73 (range 0.03 mo). Adjusted OLS coefficients for uninsured, medicaid, and private (vs medicare reference) are all within ±0.02 mo with p ≥ 0.37. No detectable association between insurance and PFS. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Receipt of feature_109 differs by feature_018 (race), suggesting racial disparities in access to a beneficial treatment.
Receipt of feature_109 differs by feature_045 (insurance), suggesting insurance-based disparities.
Receipt of the synergistic feature_109+feature_039 combination differs by race or insurance.
feature_051 (advanced-disease marker) prevalence differs by race or insurance, which would propagate any downstream PFS disparity.

analyses.result_summary: Receipt rates of feature_109: asian 10.7%, black 9.4%, hispanic 10.0%, other 9.7%, white 10.0%. Chi-square p = 0.43. No racial disparity. Same test for feature_039 by race: p = 0.26 (rates 8.6-10.5%). Hypothesis NOT supported.

Receipt rates of feature_109: medicaid 10.3%, medicare 9.9%, private 9.9%, uninsured 10.9%. Chi-square p = 0.40. Same test for feature_039 by insurance: p = 0.60 (rates 9.3-10.3%). No insurance-based disparity. Hypothesis NOT supported.

Combination receipt (feature_109 = 1 AND feature_039 = 1) rates: 0.99% (asian), 0.96% (black), 1.03% (hispanic), 0.82% (other), 1.10% (white) — chi-square p = 0.78. By insurance: 1.10% (medicaid), 1.06% (medicare), 1.02% (private), 1.20% (uninsured) — p = 0.85. The valuable combination therapy is distributed equally across race and insurance. Hypothesis NOT supported.

feature_051 prevalence by race ranges 54.4-55.4% across all five groups (chi-square p = 0.82). By insurance: 54.6-56.2% (chi-square p = 0.37). No demographic patterning of the advanced-disease marker. Hypothesis NOT supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The age effect on pfs_months is modified by ECOG status: each year of age contributes less to PFS in patients with worse (higher) ECOG (negative age × ECOG interaction).
The protective effect of feature_009 (albumin-like) on pfs_months is consistent across ECOG strata (no significant feature_009 × ECOG interaction).
The deleterious effect of feature_051 on pfs_months is uniform across ECOG strata (no clinically meaningful feature_051 × ECOG interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient age × ECOG = -0.0098 (t = -21.3, p ≈ 4e-99). Each ECOG step blunts the per-year age benefit by ~0.01 mo, so an 80-year-old with ECOG = 0 has +1.74 mo more PFS than a 70-year-old, but the same comparison at ECOG = 2 yields ~+1.55 mo. Direction confirmed (negative interaction).

analyses.code: Pearson r within each ECOG stratum; OLS with f9 + ecog + f9*ecog
analyses.result_summary: Within-stratum Pearson r between feature_009 and pfs_months: ECOG 0 r = 0.131, ECOG 1 r = 0.132, ECOG 2 r = 0.141 — essentially identical. Interaction coefficient feature_009 × ECOG = -0.017 (t = -1.94, p = 0.053) — borderline and clinically negligible. Hypothesis SUPPORTED (no meaningful effect modification).

analyses.code: Stratified means; OLS with f51 + ecog + f51*ecog
analyses.result_summary: Crude PFS difference (feature_051 = 1 vs 0) within each ECOG stratum: -0.56 mo (ECOG 0), -0.51 mo (ECOG 1), -0.47 mo (ECOG 2). Interaction coefficient feature_051 × ECOG = +0.027 (t = +3.07, p = 0.002): statistically detectable but tiny — the deleterious effect of feature_051 attenuates by only ~0.03 mo per ECOG step. Hypothesis SUPPORTED (effect is essentially uniform).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic benefit of the feature_109+feature_039 combination is consistent across race categories (no meaningful race-by-treatment interaction).
The synergistic benefit of the feature_109+feature_039 combination is consistent across insurance categories.
The combination benefit is similar in feature_051 = 0 and feature_051 = 1 patients (no large feature_109 × feature_039 × feature_051 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Combination benefit by race: asian +1.42 mo (n=30 vs 2408), black +1.76 mo (n=58 vs 4952), hispanic +0.99 mo (n=78 vs 6134), white +1.51 mo (n=355 vs 26135), other +2.74 mo (n=8 vs 806). Variation is consistent with sampling noise given small both-treatment cell sizes. The benefit is large and positive in every race. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: PFS(both=1) - PFS(neither=1) within each insurance category
analyses.result_summary: Combination benefit by insurance: medicare +1.40 mo (n=224), private +1.51 mo (n=203), medicaid +1.77 mo (n=78), uninsured +0.98 mo (n=24). All four insurance categories see a substantial positive benefit of similar magnitude. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: OLS with age + ecog + f51 + f109 + f51*f109 (separately f51*f39)
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 × feature_109 interaction = -0.017 mo (p = 0.37). feature_051 × feature_039 interaction = +0.024 mo (p = 0.21). Neither interaction term reaches significance, so the combination benefit is preserved in both advanced (feature_051 = 1) and non-advanced (feature_051 = 0) disease. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the seven dominant predictors (feature_078, feature_057, feature_009, feature_013, feature_006, feature_051, feature_092), the remaining 110+ binary and continuous features contribute little additional explanatory power for pfs_months.

The feature_013 (PSA-like) effect on pfs_months is concentrated in feature_051 = 0 patients; feature_013 carries less marginal information once feature_051 is set to 1 (positive feature_013 × feature_051 interaction).

analyses.code: Pearson r between each remaining feature and the residuals from the 7-feature model
analyses.result_summary: After fitting the 7-feature core model (R² = 0.947), residuals were correlated with every other feature. The largest absolute residual r is 0.024 (feature_067, p ≈ 6e-8) and only a handful of features cross |r| > 0.01. Adding the feature_109 × feature_039 synergy plus Gleason raises R² from 0.947 to 0.952 (Δ ≈ 0.005). The remaining 100+ features contribute essentially nothing. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: OLS with age + ecog + f51 + f13 + f13*f51
analyses.result_summary: Main effect feature_013 = -0.0179 mo per unit (in feature_051 = 0); interaction feature_013 × feature_051 = +0.0153 mo per unit (t = +59.1, p ~ 0). Implied feature_013 slope in feature_051 = 1 patients is -0.0026 mo per unit — about 7× smaller. So feature_013 chiefly drives PFS in patients without the advanced-disease marker; once feature_051 is set, its incremental prognostic value is small. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A parsimonious multivariable model containing age, ECOG, feature_051, feature_009, feature_013, feature_006, feature_092, feature_109+feature_039 with their interaction, and Gleason explains nearly all of the variance in pfs_months (R² > 0.95) and renders race and insurance non-significant after adjustment.
Most of the explainable variance is attributable to age (feature_078) alone, with ECOG (feature_057) being the second largest contributor.

analyses.code: Full OLS with 14 predictors including race + insurance dummies and feature_109*feature_039 interaction
analyses.result_summary: Final OLS R² = 0.9518. All seven prognostic continuous/ordinal predictors and feature_051 are highly significant (|t| ≥ 90). feature_109 × feature_039 = +1.44 mo (t = 66.6, p ~ 0). Gleason = -0.011 (t = -5.6). Race (nonwhite vs white) = +0.002 mo (p = 0.57); uninsured = -0.002 (p = 0.81); medicaid = -0.009 (p = 0.11). Race and insurance terms are all non-significant after adjustment. Hypothesis SUPPORTED.

Age alone: R² = 0.732. Age + ECOG: R² = 0.880 (+0.148). Adding feature_051, feature_009, feature_013, feature_006, feature_092: R² = 0.947 (+0.067). Adding feature_109 × feature_039 synergy and Gleason: R² = 0.952 (+0.005). Age dominates (~77% of total explainable variance), with ECOG a strong second contributor (~16%). Direction confirmed.
